# Audited by [V12](https://zellic.ai/)

The only autonomous auditor that finds critical bugs. Not all audits are equal, so stop paying for bad ones. Just use V12. No calls, demos, or intros.

_Note: Not all issues are guaranteed to be correct._

---

# Expired reserve list drops ACLs
**#44331**
- Severity: High
- Validity: Unreviewed

## Description

`get_reserves_list` silently treats a missing `RESERVES_LIST` key as `Vec::new(env)` instead of failing, even though that key is later used as the authoritative reserve universe for security-sensitive maintenance. The key’s TTL is only refreshed by list-specific helpers, and the identified call sites are narrow; routine reserve activity does not keep it alive. `sync_access_control_flags` rebuilds `RESERVE_WL_MAP` and `RESERVE_BL_MAP` exclusively by iterating over `get_reserves_list`, so an expired list causes both maps to be rewritten empty while the underlying per-reserve whitelist and blacklist vectors still remain in persistent storage. `is_address_whitelisted_for_reserve` treats a missing map entry as open access, and `is_address_blacklisted_for_reserve` treats it as not blacklisted, so reserve ACL enforcement is disabled for every reserve omitted from the expired list. Because `upgrade()` invokes `sync_access_control_flags` and there is also an admin `sync_access_control_flags` entrypoint, a routine maintenance action after list expiry can silently drop reserve-level access controls.

## Root Cause

`get_reserves_list` conflates “the reserve list key is missing or expired” with “the protocol has zero reserves.” Downstream ACL synchronization trusts that silent fallback as authoritative and clears consolidated security maps instead of failing closed.

## Impact

A patient attacker can wait for `RESERVES_LIST` to age out, then exploit the next legitimate upgrade or ACL resync to gain access to reserves that were supposed to be restricted by whitelist or blacklist. After that point, restricted accounts can pass reserve-level access checks for supply, borrow, withdraw, swap, flash-loan participant validation, or liquidation flows until an admin reconstructs the consolidated maps.

---

# Expiring blacklist reopens liquidation
**#44333**
- Severity: High
- Validity: Unreviewed

## Description

`set_liquidation_blacklist` stores the liquidation blacklist in `env.storage().persistent()` and only refreshes it with `extend_ttl`, so the restriction lives in expirable storage rather than a permanent access-control slot. `get_liquidation_blacklist` then converts any missing entry into `Vec::new(env)` via `unwrap_or`, and `is_blacklisted_for_liquidation` treats an empty vector as open access. That means blacklist expiry is interpreted as “no blacklist configured” instead of “configuration missing”, which silently disables the control. A previously blocked liquidator can therefore regain access simply by waiting until the stored blacklist ages out without being refreshed. Because the check is used to gate who may liquidate, this is a concrete access-control bypass rather than a harmless read inconsistency.

## Root Cause

The liquidation blacklist is stored in expirable `persistent` storage, and missing state is handled permissively. `get_liquidation_blacklist` and `is_address_blacklisted_for_liquidation` collapse “expired/missing configuration” into “no blacklist”, creating an access-control bypass after TTL expiry.

## Impact

A liquidator that governance explicitly blacklisted can wait for the stored blacklist entry to expire and then execute liquidations anyway. They can resume collecting liquidation bonuses and participating in distressed-position flows even though the admin never removed them from the blacklist.

## Proof of Concept

### Setup Script

```
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# install dependencies
rustup default stable
```

### Invalid Reason

Not exploitable on Soroban protocol 23: archived persistent entries are automatically restored on access rather than read as missing, so blacklist expiry does not turn `get_liquidation_blacklist()` into `Vec::new`. I verified this by advancing the ledger past the stored blacklist TTL in a direct `k2-kinetic-router` cargo test; the router still returned the blacklisted address and `is_blacklisted_for_liquidation` remained true.

---

# Expired index resets rewards
**#44335**
- Severity: High
- Validity: Unreviewed

## Description

`get_asset_reward_index` silently treats a missing `ASSET_REWARD_INDEX` entry as a brand-new stream by returning `AssetRewardIndex { index: RAY, last_update_timestamp: env.ledger().timestamp() }` instead of signaling that the reward index disappeared. That is unsafe because reward indices are persistent accounting state with a finite one-year TTL, not optional cache data. The contract also exposes public `get_asset_reward_config` and `get_reward_tokens` views, and both refresh only their own keys, so a patient attacker can keep a target reward configuration discoverable while letting only the index key expire. When the next `handle_action` runs, it reads the forged fresh index, `update_asset_reward_index` sees zero elapsed time, and `set_asset_reward_index` commits the reset baseline back to storage. All rewards that should have accrued since the last real index update are silently discarded for that asset/reward stream.

## Root Cause

`get_asset_reward_index` fails open: it fabricates a fresh `AssetRewardIndex` for any missing key, even though configured reward streams rely on that key as durable accounting state. This conflates an uninitialized stream with expired or lost state and lets direct callers commit a reset index instead of reverting or reinitializing through an authenticated path.

## Impact

A patient attacker can target low-activity markets and permanently erase a long interval of unclaimed incentives for all suppliers or borrowers on that stream without any privileged access. The missing emissions are never reflected in the global index again, so affected users cannot recover that yield through normal claims.

---

# Unverified DEX data drives swaps
**#44347**
- Severity: High
- Validity: Unreviewed

## Description

`swap_exact_tokens` crosses an external DEX trust boundary twice but treats both answers as authoritative. When `pair_address` is `None`, it asks `router.get_factory()` and `factory.get_pair()` for the destination pair and immediately authorizes `from_token.transfer(caller, pair_address, amount_in)` to that returned address. It then calls `router.swap_exact_tokens_for_tokens` and trusts `amounts.last()` as the actual received output, even though the safer `swap_via_handler` path in the same module measures the real `to_token` balance delta instead. `KineticRouter.initialize()` only stores `dex_router`, so both `swap_collateral` and `execute_liquidation` hit this router fallback until a factory is explicitly configured. A compromised or buggy router/factory can therefore choose where the pool's input tokens are sent and can overstate the output that downstream accounting uses.

## Root Cause

`swap_exact_tokens` treats external DEX-provided pair metadata and router-reported output as trusted sources of truth. It neither derives the pair from a trusted factory nor validates the swap by measuring the contract's real post-swap `to_token` balance delta.

## Impact

If the configured DEX dependency lies or underdelivers, a collateral swapper or liquidator can be over-credited with `to_token`/`debt_asset` while any residual balance already held by the pool silently covers the shortfall. In the same path, the sold asset can be redirected to an attacker-controlled pseudo-pair, converting the pool's swap input plus any residual output balance into attacker profit.

## Proof of Concept

### Setup Script

```
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# install dependencies
rustup default stable
```

### Invalid Reason

Not exploitable through unprivileged public entry points. The attacker cannot choose the DEX dependency: `initialize()` stores the router at deployment, and later `set_dex_router()` / `set_dex_factory()` are pool-admin-only (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/router.rs:136-165, 226-255`); custom `swap_handler` use is also admin-whitelisted only (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/access_control.rs:169-193`). So a malicious router/factory/handler requires privileged configuration or an already-compromised external dependency, not a public-user exploit of K2. Separately, the reported over-credit path only works if the router already holds enough output tokens: after the unverified swap result is returned, `swap_collateral` still must successfully transfer real `to_asset` from the router to treasury / aToken or it returns `UnderlyingTransferFailed` (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/swap.rs:214-296`), and `execute_liquidation` likewise must transfer real debt tokens from the router to the reserve / treasury / liquidator or it reverts (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/flash_loan.rs:499-557`). Without pre-existing router balances, underdelivery does not create spendable assets. If the liquidator self-funds those balances, the only remaining effect is equivalent to the already-public direct liquidation flow, which already lets any liquidator pay debt and receive collateral (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/liquidation.rs:299-318, 537-553`), and close-factor enforcement is shared across direct and prepared liquidation paths (`contracts/kinetic-router/src/liquidation.rs:10-18`).

---

# Deficit treated as available liquidity
**#44353**
- Severity: High
- Validity: Unreviewed

## Description

`update_interest_rates_and_store` recalculates reserve rates through `calculate_interest_rates_for_reserve`, which derives utilization from `total_supply` and `total_debt` and then sets `available_liquidity = total_supply - total_debt`. Both liquidation flows can burn unrecoverable debt and book it separately with `storage::add_reserve_deficit(...)` before calling this function again, so the bad debt is removed from `total_debt` without any underlying liquidity being restored. Because the rate update does not subtract the stored deficit or read the aToken’s actual underlying balance, it writes artificially low `current_variable_borrow_rate` and `current_liquidity_rate` back to storage after a bad-debt event. The borrow path itself checks real aToken cash before lending, so the protocol can remain short of underlying while this function reprices the reserve as if liquidity had returned. Once a reserve carries deficit, any borrower can keep debt there at a systematically discounted rate until treasury/admins cover the hole.

## Root Cause

The rate update logic assumes `total_supply - total_debt` is the source of truth for available liquidity. That invariant stops holding once bad debt is externalized into `reserve_deficit`, but `calculate_interest_rates_for_reserve` does not subtract the deficit or use actual underlying liquidity.

## Impact

A deficit-bearing reserve is repriced as healthier than it really is, so borrowers can open or maintain debt at undercharged interest for as long as the deficit persists. That leaks yield from suppliers and the treasury, and the leakage scales with the size and lifetime of the bad-debt deficit.

## Proof of Concept

### Test Case

```
#![cfg(test)]

use k2_a_token::{ATokenContract, ATokenContractClient};
use k2_debt_token::{DebtTokenContract, DebtTokenContractClient};
use k2_incentives::{IncentivesContract, IncentivesContractClient};
use k2_interest_rate_strategy::{InterestRateStrategyContract, InterestRateStrategyContractClient};
use k2_kinetic_router::{KineticRouterContract, KineticRouterContractClient};
use k2_pool_configurator::{PoolConfiguratorContract, PoolConfiguratorContractClient};
use k2_price_oracle::{PriceOracleContract, PriceOracleContractClient};
use k2_shared::{Asset, InitReserveParams, WAD};
use k2_treasury::{TreasuryContract, TreasuryContractClient};
use soroban_sdk::{
    contract, contractimpl,
    testutils::{Address as _, Ledger, LedgerInfo},
    token, Address, Env, String,
};

const ORACLE_ONE_USD: u128 = 1_000_000_000_000_000;
const SEQ_EXPIRY: u32 = 200_000;

#[contract]
pub struct MockReflector;

#[contractimpl]
impl MockReflector {
    pub fn decimals(_env: Env) -> u32 {
        14
    }
}

fn set_default_ledger(env: &Env) {
    env.ledger().set(LedgerInfo {
        sequence_number: 100,
        protocol_version: 23,
        timestamp: 1_700_000_000,
        network_id: Default::default(),
        base_reserve: 10,
        min_temp_entry_ttl: 10,
        min_persistent_entry_ttl: 10,
        max_entry_ttl: 3_110_400,
    });
}

pub struct Harness<'a> {
    pub env: &'a Env,
    pub admin: Address,
    pub emergency_admin: Address,
    pub liquidity_provider: Address,
    pub user: Address,
    pub router: KineticRouterContractClient<'a>,
    pub router_id: Address,
    pub oracle: PriceOracleContractClient<'a>,
    pub pool_configurator: PoolConfiguratorContractClient<'a>,
    pub treasury: TreasuryContractClient<'a>,
    pub incentives: IncentivesContractClient<'a>,
    pub underlying: Address,
    pub underlying_token: token::Client<'a>,
    pub a_token: ATokenContractClient<'a>,
    pub debt_token: DebtTokenContractClient<'a>,
}

impl<'a> Harness<'a> {
    pub fn new(env: &'a Env) -> Self {
        env.mock_all_auths();
        set_default_ledger(env);

        let admin = Address::generate(env);
        let emergency_admin = Address::generate(env);
        let liquidity_provider = Address::generate(env);
        let user = Address::generate(env);

        let reflector_id = env.register(MockReflector, ());
        let oracle_id = env.register(PriceOracleContract, ());
        let oracle = PriceOracleContractClient::new(env, &oracle_id);
        oracle.initialize(
            &admin,
            &reflector_id,
            &Address::generate(env),
            &Address::generate(env),
        );

        let treasury_id = env.register(TreasuryContract, ());
        let treasury = TreasuryContractClient::new(env, &treasury_id);
        treasury.initialize(&admin);

        let router_id = env.register(KineticRouterContract, ());
        let router = KineticRouterContractClient::new(env, &router_id);
        router.initialize(
            &admin,
            &emergency_admin,
            &oracle_id,
            &treasury_id,
            &Address::generate(env),
            &None,
        );

        let incentives_id = env.register(IncentivesContract, ());
        let incentives = IncentivesContractClient::new(env, &incentives_id);
        incentives.initialize(&admin, &router_id);

        let pool_configurator_id = env.register(PoolConfiguratorContract, ());
        let pool_configurator = PoolConfiguratorContractClient::new(env, &pool_configurator_id);
        pool_configurator.initialize(&admin, &router_id, &oracle_id);
        router.set_pool_configurator(&pool_configurator_id);

        let underlying_sac = env.register_stellar_asset_contract_v2(admin.clone());
        let underlying = underlying_sac.address();
        let underlying_token = token::Client::new(env, &underlying);
        let underlying_admin = token::StellarAssetClient::new(env, &underlying);

        underlying_admin.mint(&liquidity_provider, &100_000_000_000_000i128);
        underlying_admin.mint(&user, &100_000_000_000_000i128);

        underlying_token.approve(&liquidity_provider, &router_id, &i128::MAX, &SEQ_EXPIRY);
        underlying_token.approve(&user, &router_id, &i128::MAX, &SEQ_EXPIRY);

        let a_token_id = env.register(ATokenContract, ());
        let a_token = ATokenContractClient::new(env, &a_token_id);
        a_token.initialize(
            &admin,
            &underlying,
            &router_id,
            &String::from_str(env, "Harness aToken"),
            &String::from_str(env, "haTST"),
            &7u32,
        );

        let debt_token_id = env.register(DebtTokenContract, ());
        let debt_token = DebtTokenContractClient::new(env, &debt_token_id);
        debt_token.initialize(
            &admin,
            &underlying,
            &router_id,
            &String::from_str(env, "Harness Debt"),
            &String::from_str(env, "hdTST"),
            &7u32,
        );

        let strategy_id = env.register(InterestRateStrategyContract, ());
        let strategy = InterestRateStrategyContractClient::new(env, &strategy_id);
        strategy.initialize(
            &admin,
            &20_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &40_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &600_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &800_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
        );

        let asset = Asset::Stellar(underlying.clone());
        oracle.add_asset(&admin, &asset);
        oracle.set_manual_override(
            &admin,
            &asset,
            &Some(ORACLE_ONE_USD),
            &Some(env.ledger().timestamp() + 604_800),
        );

        let reserve_params = InitReserveParams {
            decimals: 7,
            ltv: 8000,
            liquidation_threshold: 8500,
            liquidation_bonus: 500,
            reserve_factor: 1000,
            supply_cap: 1_000_000_000_000_000,
            borrow_cap: 500_000_000_000_000,
            borrowing_enabled: true,
            flashloan_enabled: true,
        };

        router.init_reserve(
            &pool_configurator_id,
            &underlying,
            &a_token_id,
            &debt_token_id,
            &strategy_id,
            &treasury_id,
            &reserve_params,
        );

        Self {
            env,
            admin,
            emergency_admin,
            liquidity_provider,
            user,
            router,
            router_id,
            oracle,
            pool_configurator,
            treasury,
            incentives,
            underlying,
            underlying_token,
            a_token,
            debt_token,
        }
    }
}

#[test]
fn test_runtime_lending_flow_harness() {
    let env = Env::default();
    let harness = Harness::new(&env);

    let pool_liquidity = 50_000_000_000u128;
    let collateral = 20_000_000_000u128;
    let borrow_amount = 5_000_000_000u128;

    harness.router.supply(
        &harness.liquidity_provider,
        &harness.underlying,
        &pool_liquidity,
        &harness.liquidity_provider,
        &0u32,
    );

    harness.router.supply(
        &harness.user,
        &harness.underlying,
        &collateral,
        &harness.user,
        &0u32,
    );
    harness
        .router
        .set_user_use_reserve_as_coll(&harness.user, &harness.underlying, &true);

    let balance_before_borrow = harness.underlying_token.balance(&harness.user);
    harness.router.borrow(
        &harness.user,
        &harness.underlying,
        &borrow_amount,
        &1u32,
        &0u32,
        &harness.user,
    );
    let balance_after_borrow = harness.underlying_token.balance(&harness.user);
    assert_eq!(
        balance_after_borrow,
        balance_before_borrow + borrow_amount as i128,
        "borrow should transfer underlying to the user",
    );

    let account = harness.router.get_user_account_data(&harness.user);
    assert!(account.total_collateral_base > 0, "collateral should be tracked");
    assert!(account.total_debt_base > 0, "debt should be tracked");

    harness.router.repay(
        &harness.user,
        &harness.underlying,
        &u128::MAX,
        &1u32,
        &harness.user,
    );
    assert_eq!(harness.debt_token.balance(&harness.user), 0);

    harness.router.withdraw(
        &harness.user,
        &harness.underlying,
        &u128::MAX,
        &harness.user,
    );
    assert_eq!(harness.a_token.balance(&harness.user), 0);

    let final_account = harness.router.get_user_account_data(&harness.user);
    assert_eq!(final_account.total_debt_base, 0);
    assert_eq!(final_account.total_collateral_base, 0);
}

struct Reserve<'a> {
    asset: Address,
    token: token::Client<'a>,
    a_token_id: Address,
    a_token: ATokenContractClient<'a>,
    debt_token: DebtTokenContractClient<'a>,
}

struct DeficitHarness<'a> {
    env: &'a Env,
    admin: Address,
    liquidity_provider: Address,
    victim: Address,
    liquidator: Address,
    attacker: Address,
    router: KineticRouterContractClient<'a>,
    router_id: Address,
    oracle: PriceOracleContractClient<'a>,
    pool_configurator_id: Address,
    treasury_id: Address,
    strategy: InterestRateStrategyContractClient<'a>,
    collateral: Reserve<'a>,
    debt: Reserve<'a>,
}

impl<'a> DeficitHarness<'a> {
    fn new(env: &'a Env) -> Self {
        env.mock_all_auths();
        env.budget().reset_unlimited();
        set_default_ledger(env);

        let admin = Address::generate(env);
        let emergency_admin = Address::generate(env);
        let liquidity_provider = Address::generate(env);
        let victim = Address::generate(env);
        let liquidator = Address::generate(env);
        let attacker = Address::generate(env);

        let reflector_id = env.register(MockReflector, ());
        let oracle_id = env.register(PriceOracleContract, ());
        let oracle = PriceOracleContractClient::new(env, &oracle_id);
        oracle.initialize(
            &admin,
            &reflector_id,
            &Address::generate(env),
            &Address::generate(env),
        );

        let treasury_id = env.register(TreasuryContract, ());
        let treasury = TreasuryContractClient::new(env, &treasury_id);
        treasury.initialize(&admin);

        let router_id = env.register(KineticRouterContract, ());
        let router = KineticRouterContractClient::new(env, &router_id);
        router.initialize(
            &admin,
            &emergency_admin,
            &oracle_id,
            &treasury_id,
            &Address::generate(env),
            &None,
        );

        let incentives_id = env.register(IncentivesContract, ());
        let incentives = IncentivesContractClient::new(env, &incentives_id);
        incentives.initialize(&admin, &router_id);

        let pool_configurator_id = env.register(PoolConfiguratorContract, ());
        let pool_configurator = PoolConfiguratorContractClient::new(env, &pool_configurator_id);
        pool_configurator.initialize(&admin, &router_id, &oracle_id);
        router.set_pool_configurator(&pool_configurator_id);

        let strategy_id = env.register(InterestRateStrategyContract, ());
        let strategy = InterestRateStrategyContractClient::new(env, &strategy_id);
        strategy.initialize(
            &admin,
            &20_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &40_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &600_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
            &800_000_000_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
        );

        let collateral = deploy_reserve(
            env,
            &admin,
            &router_id,
            &oracle,
            &pool_configurator_id,
            &treasury_id,
            &strategy_id,
            "PoC Collateral aToken",
            "pocA",
            "PoC Collateral Debt",
            "pocdA",
        );
        let debt = deploy_reserve(
            env,
            &admin,
            &router_id,
            &oracle,
            &pool_configurator_id,
            &treasury_id,
            &strategy_id,
            "PoC Debt aToken",
            "pocB",
            "PoC DebtToken",
            "pocdB",
        );

        Self {
            env,
            admin,
            liquidity_provider,
            victim,
            liquidator,
            attacker,
            router,
            router_id,
            oracle,
            pool_configurator_id,
            treasury_id,
            strategy,
            collateral,
            debt,
        }
    }
}

fn deploy_reserve<'a>(
    env: &'a Env,
    admin: &Address,
    router_id: &Address,
    oracle: &PriceOracleContractClient<'a>,
    pool_configurator_id: &Address,
    treasury_id: &Address,
    strategy_id: &Address,
    a_name: &str,
    a_symbol: &str,
    debt_name: &str,
    debt_symbol: &str,
) -> Reserve<'a> {
    let sac = env.register_stellar_asset_contract_v2(admin.clone());
    let asset = sac.address();
    let token_client = token::Client::new(env, &asset);

    let a_token_id = env.register(ATokenContract, ());
    let a_token = ATokenContractClient::new(env, &a_token_id);
    a_token.initialize(
        admin,
        &asset,
        router_id,
        &String::from_str(env, a_name),
        &String::from_str(env, a_symbol),
        &7u32,
    );

    let debt_token_id = env.register(DebtTokenContract, ());
    let debt_token = DebtTokenContractClient::new(env, &debt_token_id);
    debt_token.initialize(
        admin,
        &asset,
        router_id,
        &String::from_str(env, debt_name),
        &String::from_str(env, debt_symbol),
        &7u32,
    );

    let asset_enum = Asset::Stellar(asset.clone());
    oracle.add_asset(admin, &asset_enum);
    oracle.set_manual_override(
        admin,
        &asset_enum,
        &Some(ORACLE_ONE_USD),
        &Some(env.ledger().timestamp() + 604_800),
    );

    let reserve_params = InitReserveParams {
        decimals: 7,
        ltv: 8000,
        liquidation_threshold: 8500,
        liquidation_bonus: 500,
        reserve_factor: 1000,
        supply_cap: 1_000_000_000_000_000,
        borrow_cap: 500_000_000_000_000,
        borrowing_enabled: true,
        flashloan_enabled: true,
    };

    KineticRouterContractClient::new(env, router_id).init_reserve(
        pool_configurator_id,
        &asset,
        &a_token_id,
        &debt_token_id,
        strategy_id,
        treasury_id,
        &reserve_params,
    );

    Reserve {
        asset,
        token: token_client,
        a_token_id,
        a_token,
        debt_token,
    }
}

fn mint_and_approve<'a>(
    env: &'a Env,
    reserve: &Reserve<'a>,
    owner: &Address,
    spender: &Address,
    amount: i128,
) {
    let sac_admin = token::StellarAssetClient::new(env, &reserve.asset);
    sac_admin.mint(owner, &amount);
    reserve.token.approve(owner, spender, &i128::MAX, &SEQ_EXPIRY);
}

fn set_price<'a>(
    env: &'a Env,
    oracle: &PriceOracleContractClient<'a>,
    admin: &Address,
    asset: &Address,
    price: u128,
) {
    let asset_enum = Asset::Stellar(asset.clone());
    oracle.reset_circuit_breaker(admin, &asset_enum);
    oracle.set_manual_override(
        admin,
        &asset_enum,
        &Some(price),
        &Some(env.ledger().timestamp() + 604_800),
    );
}

#[test]
fn test_deficit_reprices_borrow_rate_as_if_liquidity_returned() {
    const LP_LIQUIDITY: i128 = 2_0000000;
    const VICTIM_COLLATERAL: i128 = 2_0000000;
    const VICTIM_BORROW: u128 = 1_0000000;
    const ATTACKER_COLLATERAL: i128 = 100_0000000;
    const ATTACKER_BORROW: u128 = 1_0000000;
    const RESERVE_FACTOR_BPS: u128 = 1000;
    const CRASHED_PRICE: u128 = 29_000_000_000_000;

    let env = Env::default();
    let harness = DeficitHarness::new(&env);

    mint_and_approve(
        &env,
        &harness.debt,
        &harness.liquidity_provider,
        &harness.router_id,
        LP_LIQUIDITY,
    );
    harness.router.supply(
        &harness.liquidity_provider,
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &(LP_LIQUIDITY as u128),
        &harness.liquidity_provider,
        &0u32,
    );

    mint_and_approve(
        &env,
        &harness.collateral,
        &harness.victim,
        &harness.router_id,
        VICTIM_COLLATERAL,
    );
    harness.router.supply(
        &harness.victim,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        &(VICTIM_COLLATERAL as u128),
        &harness.victim,
        &0u32,
    );
    harness.router.set_user_use_reserve_as_coll(
        &harness.victim,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        &true,
    );
    harness.router.borrow(
        &harness.victim,
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &VICTIM_BORROW,
        &1u32,
        &0u32,
        &harness.victim,
    );

    set_price(
        &env,
        &harness.oracle,
        &harness.admin,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        CRASHED_PRICE,
    );
    let unhealthy = harness.router.get_user_account_data(&harness.victim);
    assert!(unhealthy.health_factor < WAD / 2, "victim must be fully liquidatable");

    mint_and_approve(
        &env,
        &harness.debt,
        &harness.liquidator,
        &harness.router_id,
        (VICTIM_BORROW as i128) * 2,
    );
    harness.router.liquidation_call(
        &harness.liquidator,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &harness.victim,
        &VICTIM_BORROW,
        &false,
    );

    assert_eq!(
        harness.debt.debt_token.balance(&harness.victim),
        0,
        "bad debt liquidation burns the victim debt and moves the shortfall into reserve_deficit",
    );

    let deficit = harness.router.get_reserve_deficit(&harness.debt.asset);
    assert!(deficit > 0, "liquidation must create a deficit on the debt reserve");

    mint_and_approve(
        &env,
        &harness.collateral,
        &harness.attacker,
        &harness.router_id,
        ATTACKER_COLLATERAL,
    );
    harness.router.supply(
        &harness.attacker,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        &(ATTACKER_COLLATERAL as u128),
        &harness.attacker,
        &0u32,
    );
    harness.router.set_user_use_reserve_as_coll(
        &harness.attacker,
        &harness.collateral.asset,
        &true,
    );
    let attacker_account = harness.router.get_user_account_data(&harness.attacker);
    assert!(
        attacker_account.available_borrows_base >= 1_000_000_000_000_000_000u128,
        "attacker collateral should support borrowing one full debt token",
    );
    harness.router.borrow(
        &harness.attacker,
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &ATTACKER_BORROW,
        &1u32,
        &0u32,
        &harness.attacker,
    );

    let stored_reserve = harness.router.get_reserve_data(&harness.debt.asset);
    let actual_cash = harness.debt.token.balance(&harness.debt.a_token_id) as u128;
    let outstanding_debt = harness.debt.debt_token.balance(&harness.attacker) as u128;
    let total_supply = harness.debt.a_token.balance(&harness.liquidity_provider) as u128;
    let modeled_available = total_supply - outstanding_debt;

    assert_eq!(
        modeled_available,
        actual_cash + deficit,
        "router still models deficit as if it were liquid cash",
    );

    let incorrect_rates = harness.strategy.calculate_interest_rates(
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &modeled_available,
        &outstanding_debt,
        &RESERVE_FACTOR_BPS,
    );
    let correct_rates = harness.strategy.calculate_interest_rates(
        &harness.debt.asset,
        &actual_cash,
        &outstanding_debt,
        &RESERVE_FACTOR_BPS,
    );

    assert_eq!(
        stored_reserve.current_variable_borrow_rate,
        incorrect_rates.variable_borrow_rate,
        "stored borrow rate follows total_supply - total_debt, not actual debt-reserve cash",
    );
    assert_eq!(
        stored_reserve.current_liquidity_rate,
        incorrect_rates.liquidity_rate,
        "stored liquidity rate is derived from the same overstated liquidity input",
    );
    assert!(
        correct_rates.variable_borrow_rate > stored_reserve.current_variable_borrow_rate,
        "if the reserve deficit were subtracted, borrowers would pay a higher rate",
    );
    assert!(
        correct_rates.liquidity_rate > stored_reserve.current_liquidity_rate,
        "suppliers also earn less because utilization is understated",
    );
}
```

### Setup Script

```
#!/bin/bash
set -e

# install dependencies
rustup default stable
```
